title: Robustly Roasted Mexican Poblano Burrito
description: A kid-friendly Mexican dish featuring roasted poblano peppers and gochujang, cooked in a clay pot and wrapped in a whole30-friendly burrito.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
Roast the poblano peppers in the oven for 30 minutes, then peel off the skin and chop them into small pieces.
Cook the ground beef in a clay pot over medium heat, breaking it up into small pieces as it cooks.
Add the gochujang, cauliflower rice, avocado oil, lime juice, and salt to the clay pot and stir to combine.
Add the roasted poblano peppers to the clay pot and stir to combine.
Warm the whole30 compliant tortillas by wrapping them in a damp paper towel and microwaving for 20 seconds.
Assemble the burritos by spooning the mixture from the clay pot onto a tortilla and rolling it up.
Serve the burritos hot, garnished with additional lime juice and salt if desired.
